activities.markdown:
# The Balanced Drummer's Practice

**30-minute daily routine:**
| Time | Focus |
|------|-------|
| 5 min | Warm-up (single strokes, slow) |
| 5 min | Rudiments (current rudiment, build speed) |
| 5 min | Independence (add new pattern) |
| 10 min | Songs / grooves (material you're learning) |
| 5 min | Free play (improvise, have fun) |

Always use a metronome. Track your max clean tempo for each skill — progress is motivating.

activities.question: Why should every practice session end with free play?
choices: Free play doesn't require a metronome
It applies skills musically and keeps drumming enjoyable
It's required to cool down muscles
To test if the skills transferred
activities.explanation: Free play lets you apply what you practiced in an enjoyable, musical context. It reinforces skills naturally and — most importantly — keeps drumming fun. Enjoyment sustains long-term practice.
